approval_phase_notes.phase_1: Founder approves every item via the Dashboard queue, regardless of content type or confidence.
approval_phase_notes.phase_2: Content types listed in auto_publish_eligible below auto-publish when confidence exceeds their threshold. Everything else queues.
approval_phase_notes.phase_3: Only market_updates, major announcements, new content formats, and low-confidence items reach the founder.

quality_score.note: educational_value carries the highest weight by explicit founder rule — see brain/ceo.md non-negotiable principles.

busy.note: Never lowers the bar for market_updates or new formats, regardless of delta.

manual.note: Hard override — takes precedence over approval_phase and every other mode.

observation_intelligence.note: A video's views vs. the average of other recently observed videos, same run. >= outperform_ratio routes to Executive (Daily Briefing); <= underperform_ratio routes to Department: writer. See departments/intelligence/OBSERVATION_INTELLIGENCE_DESIGN.md and pipeline/lib/observation-intelligence.ts. Both the TikTok source's own wording and the routing rule read these same two numbers, so they can never disagree with each other. confidence_* (M2.6, Emerging Playbooks): computeConfidence() reads these to score a pattern's occurrences deterministically — high needs both enough occurrences AND enough real time span (a burst of same-day posts isn't the same evidence as a pattern proven over time); below confidence_medium_min_occurrences is low. recent_activity_* (M2.8): read only by daily-briefing.ts's Recent Activity section (presentation layer, not routing) — window_hours is how long an observation counts as recent; min_age_hours_for_comparison is how long before it even attempts an above/below-baseline claim rather than saying 'too early to judge'. Observation Intelligence still reasons about every observation immediately regardless of these — Recent Activity is a second, independent view over the same data, not a gate in front of it.

budget.note: See architecture doc Section 6 for the tool-by-tool cost reasoning.
